ocr.text: The data presented in the accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of the population dynamics of Stomoxys
calcitrans, commonly known as the stable fly, within specific farmyard habitats. This species is of significant concern in
agricultural settings due to its role as a pest affecting livestock health and productivity.
Species  Site Habitat  Frame Colour  Mean No. of Flies per Week  Confidence Intervals
The table highlights key metrics, including the mean number of flies per week, which is reported as 20.29, along with the
associated confidence intervals ranging from 9.98 to 41.25. This statistic underscores the variability in fly populations that can
be encountered in farm environments and emphasizes the need for targeted pest management strategies. Furthermore, the
inclusion of frame colour as a parameter suggests that visual attractants may play a role in monitoring and controlling stable fly
populations. The insights derived from this data can aid in understanding the ecological factors influencing fly abundance and
inform best practices for mitigating their impact on both livestock and human health. Overall, this analysis serves as a vital
resource for researchers and practitioners in the field of entomology and agricultural management, facilitating informed
